justifications.completeness: The extraction misses one of two pedestrians and three of four annotated vehicles, so coverage of annotated objects is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides several semantic attributes like type, motion status, age, and door/trunk state but omits object IDs and contains at least one incorrect motion label and several missing vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are detailed and plausible for the detected objects but many objects lack any spatial relation due to missing detections.
justifications.overall: The extraction supplies useful semantic detail for detected entities but significant omissions and some incorrect labels limit its overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and two of five vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the few detected entities the model provides multiple attributes (actions, context, type, motion, doors) but most scene entities are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided plausible relations for detected objects but relations are missing for most ground truth objects, reducing overall coverage.
justifications.overall: Extraction includes useful attribute detail for detected objects but suffers from low recall and incomplete spatial coverage, yielding moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses all pedestrians and several vehicles, reporting only two vehicles and omitting most ground-truth objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some vehicle attributes and a store mention but omits pedestrian semantics and many motion/status details, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives a couple of relative positions for vehicles but ignores pedestrian locations and many inter-object relations present in ground truth.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few scene elements but largely omits pedestrians and many vehicles, so it is substantially incomplete and partially incorrect.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists three vehicles but misses two ground-truth vehicles and fails to detect any pedestrians present in the frame.
justifications.semantic_richness: It supplies useful per-vehicle attributes (motion, trunk, doors) but omits person detections and finer contextual labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations for the detected vehicles are plausible and relevant, but relations for the missing vehicles and the two pedestrians are absent.
justifications.overall: Partial but useful vehicle-level information is provided, yet omission of all pedestrians and some vehicles results in a moderate overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian out of seven and three vehicles out of six, missing the majority of actors in the scene.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes (type, motion, age) for some entities but omits many ground-truth details such as multiple pedestrians, carrying status, communicative actions, and exact simple contexts.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial claims but several are imprecise or unsupported by the ground truth, for example asserting a pedestrian is at a pedestrian crossing and referencing store locations not reflected in annotations.
justifications.overall: Low recall of actors combined with partial and sometimes inaccurate semantic and spatial information yields a weak overall extraction for this frame.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles (4 of 11 total annotated objects), so the extraction misses the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the few detected entities the model provides useful attributes like action and age, but it misses important labels (e.g., transporting, communicative) for several ground truth persons and mislabels vehicle motion.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided and plausible for the detected entities, but most objects lack relations and vehicle location/motion context is inconsistent with ground truth.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is low to moderate due to significant missing detections and some incorrect vehicle motion information despite reasonable attribute detail for the few detected objects.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, missing multiple people and a parked vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) but several attribute values are incorrect or mismatched to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for detected entities but contains a wrong relation for a jaywalking pedestrian and omits the parked vehicle location.
justifications.overall: Partial detection with moderate semantic coverage but notable omissions and several incorrect labels reduce reliability for driving decisions.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only two pedestrians and two vehicles while the ground truth contains five persons and five vehicles, missing the majority of objects and several important attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides a reasonable set of semantic labels (actions, contexts, age, motion status, door/trunk states) and a scene description but omits some ground-truth communicative cues and does not map items to track IDs.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some localized relations are provided (e.g., near DVD shop, center of the road) which are useful, but many objects lack spatial relations and the provided relations are not clearly validated against ground-truth positions.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers useful semantic detail for a subset of objects but is incomplete in object coverage, misses several ground-truth attributes, and provides uneven spatial information, resulting in a below-average overall result.

justifications.completeness: Many pedestrians and several vehicles from the ground truth are missing or not matched in the extraction, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction reports multiple attribute types per agent (actions, context, age) but misses or mislabels important details such as a communicative group and several walking actors, yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are provided (e.g., inside store, near entrance) but they are inconsistent with provided ground truth contexts and many objects lack spatial relations, limiting usefulness.
justifications.overall: Due to low agent coverage, some incorrect or missing labels, and partial spatial information the extraction quality is below average.

justifications.completeness: Extraction reports only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles, missing several actors and key motion labels like moving vehicles, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains many semantic fields (age, communicative, transporting, trunk/doors) indicating good richness, but several attributes are incorrect or omitted (e.g., missing 'talking in group' and jaywalking), reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but several are inaccurate or incomplete (mislabeling on-road pedestrians as sidewalk and failing to mark jaywalking), so spatial accuracy is poor.
justifications.overall: Given moderate attribute coverage but low recall of actors and notable spatial/motion errors, the overall extraction quality is below average.

justifications.completeness: Only 3 of 7 ground-truth pedestrians and 1 of 4 ground-truth vehicles are reported, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides some attributes (age, atomic_action, simple_context) but omits important labels present in the ground truth such as communicative actions and jaywalking, limiting semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few local relations are given (next to, farther down, parked near sidewalk) but critical spatial/contextual relations from the ground truth are missing or ambiguous, reducing usefulness for scene understanding.
justifications.overall: Due to low object coverage and missed key semantic and spatial details, the extraction quality is poor overall.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 pedestrians and did not report the two moving vehicles from the ground truth, so a substantial number of objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, age, transport states) but misses key contextual labels like jaywalking, mislabels a senior as adult, and omits motion status for important vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some plausible relations such as middle of the street and near the store, but omits spatial relations for most ground truth entities and lacks precision about crossing locations.
justifications.overall: Moderate-low quality overall because several pedestrians and moving vehicles are missing and some attributes are incorrect or incomplete, affecting situational awareness.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles but missed two pedestrians and one vehicle and did not mark jaywalking for the relevant people.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many per-entity attributes (actions, context, age, vehicle states) showing good richness but it mislabels important contextual categories and vehicle type.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial phrases but they are coarse and fail to capture critical road versus sidewalk and jaywalking distinctions present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Moderate coverage and attribute detail but omissions of entities and incorrect contextual/spatial labels reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected far fewer pedestrians than the five annotated and missed or mischaracterized at least one vehicle, so entity coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (age, actions, doors) but several key semantics are incorrect (most pedestrians labeled standing instead of walking) reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for reported objects which adds context but relations are incomplete and sometimes inconsistent with ground truth motion and locations.
justifications.overall: Overall moderate-low quality due to missing pedestrians and incorrect action/motion labels despite decent attribute coverage and some spatial relations.

justifications.completeness: Extracted fewer pedestrians (2 of 4) and only two of three vehicles, and misclassified at least one pedestrian's action, indicating limited coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (age, communicative, transporting, spatial relations) but misses critical labels like jaywalking and mislabels actions, yielding moderate semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives spatial relations for objects but some are vague or not matching ground truth (e.g., missing 'on road' jaywalking relations and adding unverified relations), so partially accurate.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is partially useful with moderate semantic detail but incomplete detection and some incorrect labels, resulting in an average quality.

justifications.completeness: Only two of four ground-truth persons and one of two ground-truth vehicles are reported, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction captures a variety of attributes (actions, age, doors/trunk, spatial relations) for detected entities but misses multiple objects and mislabels vehicle motion.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided and sometimes informative (near shop, near/behind cones) but cannot be fully verified against ground truth and the vehicle relation conflicts with the vehicle motion labels.
justifications.overall: Moderate attribute detail for detected entities is outweighed by poor recall and at least one critical mislabeling of vehicle motion, resulting in a below-average overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: Missed one person (sitting and biking) and two vehicles including a stopped vehicle, so several ground-truth objects are not reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful attributes (actions, age, door/trunk states) for reported objects but misses key semantics like the biking/sitting person and jaywalking context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives plausible local relations for the reported objects (right sidewalk, left side, behind another car) but omits relations for the missing objects and the illegal crossing relation.
justifications.overall: Partially correct and semantically informative for detected objects but incomplete coverage and missed critical actors reduce overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies only 3 pedestrians out of 8 ground-truth persons and one vehicle (with no track associations), so coverage of agents is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides multiple attributes per agent (actions, age, context, door/trunk states) but misses critical labels like jaywalking and incorrectly reports the vehicle as stopped instead of moving.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are provided (near shop, center, on the road ahead) but key spatial contexts from the ground truth such as 'walking on the road' versus 'walking along the side' and the jaywalking relation are inconsistently captured or missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially informative and attribute-rich but suffers from low agent coverage, some incorrect labels, and incomplete spatial/contextual mapping, yielding moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses all pedestrians and only identifies two of five ground-truth vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides useful attributes (type, motion_status, trunk and door states and brief spatial hints) but omits pedestrian attributes and many vehicles and sometimes uses imprecise type labeling.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible left-side spatial relations for the two reported vehicles, but lacks relations for the other vehicles and any relations involving the two pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Due to major omissions of pedestrians and several vehicles despite correct attribute extraction for the two reported vehicles, the overall quality is low to moderate.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but misclassified one person and omitted a parked vehicle, resulting in partial coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields including age and vehicle state, but some labels are incorrect or unsupported by ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers explicit spatial relations but several are incorrect relative to ground truth and the parked vehicle relation is missing.
justifications.overall: Partial detection and moderate attribute detail are outweighed by incorrect actions and a missing vehicle, giving an overall below average result.

justifications.completeness: Detected several vehicles and one pedestrian but missed one person (biking/sitting) from the ground truth and one parked vehicle, so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Offers many attribute fields (type, motion status, age) that add semantic detail but includes irrelevant or unverified fields (e.g., trunk/doors for a bicycle) and some likely incorrect associations.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides plausible spatial relations for most detections, but these relations are not validated against the ground truth and one relation (near potted plants) appears extraneous.
justifications.overall: A partially useful extraction that supplies helpful attributes and relations but misses objects and contains some incorrect or ungrounded details, limiting reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but only one vehicle out of four, missing the moving vehicle and other parked vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes (age, actions, transport, doors) but some labels are incorrect or non-specific reducing useful semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations provided but one pedestrian is placed on the sidewalk contrary to ground truth and several vehicles lack any spatial tagging.
justifications.overall: Moderate coverage of pedestrians and attribute richness but important errors in actions, spatial placement, and missing vehicles lower overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses most parked vehicles from ground truth and misclassifies the bicyclist as a vehicle, so many relevant objects are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attribute fields like motion_status and age but many are incorrect or left unspecified, reducing their utility.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives reasonable spatial descriptions for the person and truck that align with the scene, though not exhaustive for all vehicles.
justifications.overall: Some useful attributes and spatial cues are present but critical omissions and misclassifications lead to a below-average overall extraction.

justifications.completeness: Detects the primary bicyclist and some parked bicycles but misses multiple other parked vehicles and provides no object IDs to match GT entries.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attributes (motion_status, age, contexts, spatial_relations) offering rich semantics but mislabels the atomic action, applies trunk/door fields inappropriately to bicycles, and omits some GT attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations for the bicyclist and parked bicycles are accurate and match the scene description, though relations are absent for several other GT vehicles.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate extraction that captures core actors and useful attributes but is incomplete in object coverage and contains semantic assignment errors.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses several ground-truth people and vehicles, reporting only 4 of 8 persons and 1 of 3 vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes many attribute fields but omits or mislabels several important semantic tags from ground truth such as communicative behaviors and transporting for multiple persons and misclassifies a vehicle type.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but several are inaccurate or missing (eg crosswalk versus jaywalking distinctions and many relative positions), reducing spatial fidelity.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides a partial high-level description with some attribute detail but misses many entities and contains several semantic and spatial errors, yielding limited utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction contains only 3 pedestrians versus 7 ground-truth persons and only one vehicle versus three, so many objects are missing or unmatched.
justifications.semantic_richness: Many useful attributes are reported (actions, context, transporting, age), indicating good richness, but several attribute assignments (e.g., action labels and contexts) are incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are plausible for the reported objects, but relations are sparse, not provided for many ground-truth entities, and not verified against all objects.
justifications.overall: The extraction is attribute-rich but suffers from low object coverage and several incorrect labels, yielding a moderate-to-low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction detects several pedestrians and a vehicle but misses multiple ground-truth persons and one parked vehicle and omits several annotated behaviors like carrying, pushing, and biking.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides basic labels (actions, age, simple contexts) but fails to capture many ground-truth attributes such as transporting types, communicative cues, and jaywalking/biking contexts.
justifications.spatial_relations: Extraction supplies useful relative locations (near cones, ahead, next to) for most detected agents, though these are qualitative and some scene relationships from the ground truth are not corroborated.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance—correctly identifies several actors and basic attributes but misses important actors and semantic details, reducing reliability for safety-critical perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only two pedestrians and two vehicles while the ground truth contains seven persons and four vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The model provides a reasonable set of attributes (actions, context, transporting, motion status, door/trunk) but some labels contradict the ground truth and many objects lack any attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations match the scene (one pedestrian walking along the side and a vehicle on the road) but several actor placements are incorrect or absent and relations for most objects are missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a few correct attributes and relations but misses the majority of objects and contains notable labeling errors, yielding limited overall coverage and reliability.

justifications.completeness: Many annotated pedestrians and vehicles from the ground truth are missing and several required attributes (e.g., transporting types, some objects) are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes some attribute fields but misses or mislabels important semantics such as carrying/pushing, communicative cues, and correct vehicle motion statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few spatial relations are correctly reported (vehicle near barriers, pedestrian-to-pedestrian relations) but key locational distinctions like on-road versus sidewalk are often incorrect.
justifications.overall: Coverage is limited and multiple attribute errors reduce reliability, though some spatial cues and attributes are correctly captured.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits at least one pedestrian and two parked vehicles from the ground truth and misses a transporting label, so coverage of entities and attributes is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains many semantic fields (actions, age, spatial relations, vehicle state) showing good breadth, but it fails to capture important labels like jaywalking and a pushing transport action.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some reasonable spatial relations are provided and the stopped vehicle is localized, but the system misses relations for omitted vehicles and does not reflect jaywalking positions accurately, yielding partial correctness.
justifications.overall: Due to missed entities, some incorrect or missing contextual labels, and only partial spatial accuracy, the extraction quality is below average.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing the majority of ground-truth agents.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields (actions, context, transporting, age) but misses or mislabels several transport actions and fails to report biking and sitting instances present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides relative position descriptions that are sometimes plausible but at times vague or speculative and not consistently aligned with the ground-truth annotations.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers some useful semantic detail but suffers from low recall and multiple attribute errors, limiting its reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Missing two of four annotated persons and two of three annotated vehicles, so a large portion of ground truth entities are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes several attribute types (actions, age, motion status) but fails to capture important labels like transporting/pushing and complex contextual actions.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides some spatial tags but they are partially inaccurate or inconsistent with ground truth and many actors lack spatial relations.
justifications.overall: Partially useful extraction with some correct attributes but significant omissions and inaccuracies reduce its reliability for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two of six pedestrians and one of four vehicles, missing most objects and many annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like age and actions but misses key labels such as transporting, complex contexts, and several object attributes, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial relations that match the scene but omits many positional details and mislabels or fails to relate several actors and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Partial and occasionally accurate extraction but largely incomplete and lacking important semantic details needed for reliable driving scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, missing several people and one vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes several attributes (actions, age, doors/trunk) but misses key context like pushing and getting on a vehicle and mislabels or omits some objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides spatial relations for listed entities and correctly notes at least one sidewalk versus road relation, but several actors are missing so spatial coverage is incomplete.
justifications.overall: Extraction contains useful details but is incomplete and misses important behaviors and objects, yielding a below-average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 pedestrians out of 10 and 1 vehicle out of 5 from the ground truth, so coverage is severely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides multiple attribute fields (actions, context, transporting, age) which adds semantic detail, but several attributes are incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth and many entities are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some plausible spatial relations are provided (near barriers, near officer), offering useful context, but relations are sparse, partially behavioral rather than strictly spatial, and not verifiable for all ground truth objects.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some correct and useful semantic details but is noisy and incomplete, limiting its reliability for autonomous driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only three of six ground-truth pedestrians and one vehicle, missing multiple persons and thus incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a variety of attributes (actions, context, carrying, age) beyond the ground truth but includes incorrect labels (carrying vs pushing, officer identification, vehicle type), so moderately rich but partially inaccurate.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations that partially match the scene (sidewalk and near sidewalk) but contains incorrect claims (crosswalk and directing officer) and omits some pedestrian positions.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: captures some entities and attributes but misses many pedestrians and has several semantic and spatial errors.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth persons and vehicles are missing from the extraction: 3 of 7 people and 1 of 4 vehicles are reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (actions, simple context, age, vehicle door/trunk status) but several labels are incorrect or inconsistent with the ground truth, such as biking subjects labeled as walking.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are reasonable (a parked van near the fence) but many agents lack precise placement and some relations contradict the ground truth or are ambiguous.
justifications.overall: The output is structured and contains useful semantic fields but suffers from low recall and several incorrect labels, reducing its reliability for perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only two pedestrians and two vehicles while the ground truth contains six persons and six vehicles, so many actors and instance-level details are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM reports a variety of attributes (age, actions, types, motion status) showing moderate richness, but several attributes are incorrect, missing for many objects, or labeled as 'none of the above'.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reported spatial cues (e.g., on the right, near barriers, being ridden) are generally useful and plausible, but they are coarse and not tied to the full set of ground-truth instances.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction shows useful semantic variety and some correct relations but suffers from low recall and multiple attribute mismatches limiting its reliability.

justifications.completeness: Many objects are missing or under-detected: only 3 of 6 pedestrians and 2 vehicles reported, and several parked/moving vehicles and pedestrians were omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (actions, contexts, age, motion status), but some attributes are incorrect or inconsistent (cyclist atomic action labeled none, senior age missed).
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for reported entities and are generally plausible, but important relations like a person walking on the road and relations for many vehicles are missing or incomplete.
justifications.overall: Moderate semantic detail is present but overall performance is limited by low recall and several factual errors, yielding an average-quality extraction.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures a few actors (three pedestrians and two bicycles) but misses most ground-truth persons and vehicles, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction supplies some attributes like type, motion_status, context, and ages but omits key atomic actions for cyclists and defaults many fields, reducing semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are generally plausible for the detected actors, but many actors lack any spatial relations due to missing detections, limiting overall spatial completeness.
justifications.overall: The extraction has some correct high-level labels and plausible spatial cues but misses most ground-truth objects and contains several semantic errors, yielding a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports 3 pedestrians out of 7 and 2 vehicles out of 3 from the ground truth, missing multiple people and one vehicle so recall is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute types (actions, context, transporting, age) but contains errors and omissions such as mislabeling a senior as adult, missing a carrying event, and an unsupported pulling label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several plausible spatial relations are provided (near truck, near cones, near pedestrian), but relations are sparse, not fully verifiable against the ground truth, and include possible false positives.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall utility driven by decent semantic coverage but limited by low detection completeness and some incorrect attribute assignments.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 5 pedestrians and the vehicle, missing three people and key transporting annotations so overall coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attributes per entity (actions, context, age, vehicle state) but misses the 'carrying with both hands' transport label and mislabels a senior as an adult, reducing semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes relevant relations (pedestrian near truck, truck near cones) but does not provide relations for all pedestrians and lacks more precise localization.
justifications.overall: A partially accurate extraction with correct vehicle details and some pedestrian attributes but notable omissions in detection count, transport annotations, and age accuracy limit reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 8 pedestrians and one vehicle, so many entities are missing despite correct vehicle detection.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes (walking, age as adult, vehicle state) are provided but important labels like a senior, a person carrying items, and a person talking on phone are missed.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction supplies plausible local relations (near truck, next to sidewalk, ahead/behind) but they are sparse and not fully verified against ground truth.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: a few correct detections and reasonable relations but many pedestrians and key semantic labels are missing.

justifications.completeness: Partially complete: it identifies two pedestrians and one vehicle but misses a walking senior and does not map all ground-truth actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: Moderately rich: it provides scene context, actions, ages, and vehicle attributes but omits one person's age and simple_context and includes some possibly inferred attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Partially accurate: it offers several spatial relations for actors and the vehicle which add useful detail but some relations are unverified and one pedestrian is missing.
justifications.overall: Overall moderate performance with correct core actions and vehicle attributes but missing one person and some contextual details, yielding a satisfactory but incomplete extraction.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies only 3 of the 6 ground-truth pedestrians and omits key actors and behaviors (e.g., a person carrying items and one talking on the phone), so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: While many attribute fields are present, several important attributes are missing or mislabeled (transporting and communicative states and some simple_contexts), so the semantic detail is incomplete and partially incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are plausible and relevant for the reported pedestrians (near cones, umbrella, shops) but are coarse, not provided for all persons, and lack precise reference to ground-truth IDs, so accuracy is mixed.
justifications.overall: The extraction shows some useful information and plausible spatial cues but misses many pedestrians and key attributes, yielding a low-to-moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports 2 pedestrians versus 6 in the ground truth and therefore misses the majority of annotated persons and some attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output captures basic actions and age but omits important attributes such as transporting (carrying with both hands) and communicative signals (talking on phone) for several ground-truth persons.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some useful spatial cues are provided (near traffic cones, past shops, near end of street) but several annotated contextual relations like exiting a building or walking on the road are missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains a few correct elements but is incomplete and misses multiple persons and key semantic attributes, resulting in low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 6 pedestrians and no vehicles from the ground truth, so recall is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attribute fields (actions, transporting, age) for detected people but misses many contextual labels and misrepresents at least one simple context, so richness is moderate.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible local relations for the two detections but omits spatial relations for the majority of ground-truth persons and all vehicles, limiting usefulness.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat detailed descriptions for a small subset of actors with low recall and some labeling errors, resulting in generally low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Only one of five ground-truth persons was detected and a vehicle was falsely reported, so many objects are missing and there are false positives.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies several attribute labels for the detected entities but omits most persons and their correct attributes while inventing detailed vehicle properties that do not exist in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some plausible local relations are provided for the single detected pedestrian but relations for the other pedestrians are missing and a non-existent vehicle relation is reported.
justifications.overall: Significant omissions of multiple pedestrians and a false positive vehicle result in low overall agreement with the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two persons, missed one person entirely, failed to capture transporting and communicative attributes, and incorrectly reported a vehicle that is not in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some basic attributes like atomic action and age but omits or contradicts key semantic labels such as carrying and talking, reducing overall semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reports spatial relations for the detected person and a vehicle but these do not align with ground truth and a person with a road relation is missing, giving poor spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains significant omissions and a false positive vehicle, with mismatched semantic and spatial details, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses one of two people and fails to capture the transporting action while the vehicle is represented but with differing attribute details.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, context, age, vehicle type) but several are incorrect or missing such as transporting and an incorrect age label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible spatial relations for the detected objects that align with the scene but omits relations for the missing person and may be imprecise.
justifications.overall: Partial and moderately detailed extraction but omits a person and key action leading to only moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two of four pedestrians and no ground-truth vehicles, missing multiple instances and many object-level annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captured basic attributes like atomic actions and age for the detected people but omitted or incorrectly labeled communicative and many transporting attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations for the extracted entities but these relations do not match ground-truth annotations and do not compensate for missing objects.
justifications.overall: Partial correct detections and some attribute extraction are outweighed by missed objects, incorrect vehicle identification, and missing key communicative and transporting labels, resulting in low overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Covers some pedestrians but misses two persons and most vehicles from the ground truth, resulting in low overall object coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes basic action, simple context, and age attributes but omits key communicative and transporting labels present in the ground truth and mislabels several actions.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides several spatial tags but they are not verifiable against the ground truth and some relations appear inconsistent with annotated behaviors.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a subset of actors and some attributes but has significant omissions and mismatches across objects, actions, communicative states, and vehicles.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two vehicles and missed the pedestrian and most annotated vehicles, so coverage of ground truth objects is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides useful per-vehicle attributes such as type, motion status and open/closed states but omits person attributes and many vehicles, limiting overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: The model supplies specific spatial relations for the two detected vehicles which are informative but not comprehensive or validated against all ground truth objects.
justifications.overall: Some useful attribute-level information is present but the extraction misses most objects including the pedestrian and several vehicles, resulting in low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 3 vehicles and no pedestrians, so many ground-truth entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides vehicle motion and door/trunk states plus scene context, but omits all person attributes, track IDs, and one vehicle instance.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives plausible left/right parking relations for detected trucks but misses relations for the third vehicle and for pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Partial vehicle-level detail but fails to capture pedestrians and one vehicle, limiting overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of two pedestrians and one of two vehicles, missing the second person and one vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes like walking and adult for the detected person and closed trunk and doors for the truck are correct, but the extraction misses the second person and additional contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided reasonable spatial relations for the visible objects such as parked on the right, but omitted relations for the missed pedestrian and lacks frame-level reference consistency.
justifications.overall: Partly accurate with correct attributes for detected objects but incomplete due to missing objects and some contextual information.

justifications.completeness: Detected two vehicles and no pedestrians while ground truth contains one person and seven vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides motion and door/trunk state for the detected vehicles and simple spatial hints but omits identities, person attributes, and most objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes specific relations for the two detected trucks but lacks broader, consistent spatial relations for the other vehicles and the pedestrian.
justifications.overall: Extraction contains some useful attribute labels for a couple of vehicles but misses most objects and the pedestrian, yielding limited overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians and the parked bicycle but misclassified one person's actions and missed the shop-closing context, so partially complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields beyond the ground truth such as age, communicative, transporting, and spatial relations, increasing richness despite some incorrect or generic values.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations that add useful context, but these relations cannot be confirmed by the provided ground truth and some appear inconsistent with the annotated action of the second person closing a shop.
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially accurate and informative with good attribute coverage and spatial descriptions but contains key semantic errors for one person and missing shop-closing context, leading to a moderate overall score.

justifications.completeness: Many pedestrians and both vehicles from the ground truth are missing in the extraction; only a few crosswalk pedestrians are reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides multiple attribute fields for each pedestrian but mislabels ages and omits communicative labels and other nuanced context present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial/context labels are present but many pedestrians are incorrectly labeled as crossing when the ground truth shows most are walking along the side of the road, and vehicle relations are absent.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some attributes for a few actors but is substantially incomplete and contains notable semantic and spatial misclassifications.

justifications.completeness: Detected and labeled the pedestrian with correct action and attributes but omitted the parked vehicle and did not provide a tracking identifier, so the extraction is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a useful scene description and multiple pedestrian attributes (action, context, age, communicative and transporting status) adding semantic detail beyond the ground truth, though it misses vehicle information and an explicit track id.
justifications.spatial_relations: Supplies plausible spatial relations for the pedestrian which add value, but one relation references an unverified landmark and there is no ground-truth spatial annotation to fully confirm correctness.
justifications.overall: Strong semantic detail for the pedestrian but missing the parked vehicle and a tracking id reduces overall completeness and reliability of the extraction.

justifications.completeness: Detected only five pedestrians and one vehicle while ground truth lists over forty persons and a moving vehicle, so many entities and the correct vehicle motion are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction reports multiple semantic attributes (actions, context, transporting, age) but omits many communicative labels, introduces an incorrect transporting claim, and lacks finer contextual distinctions present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided reasonable coarse relations (crosswalk, left/right sidewalk, behind crosswalk) for the detected actors, but relations are unverified for the majority of missing pedestrians and are somewhat coarse.
justifications.overall: Some useful attributes and coarse spatial info are present, but severe under-detection of pedestrians, incorrect vehicle motion, and a few wrong attribute assignments substantially reduce overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two ground-truth pedestrians and added a spurious vehicle, so coverage of relevant entities is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (age, motion_status, doors_open, spatial_relations) but several values are incorrect or irrelevant to the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial descriptions but they do not match the ground-truth contexts (walking along the side of the road and closing) and include a false-positive vehicle relation.
justifications.overall: The extraction is incomplete and contains misclassifications and an extraneous object, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only 6 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while the ground truth contains around 25 people and 2 vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many attribute types (atomic action, context, communicative, transporting, age) which increases richness, but several attributes are incorrect or omitted compared to ground truth (e.g., missing communicative labels, wrong transporting and age details).
justifications.spatial_relations: Some pairwise relations and coarse positions are given, but relations are sparse, incomplete, and do not cover most pedestrians or consistently match ground truth reference objects.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures useful attribute categories and a few correct labels but has low coverage and several attribute and relation errors, yielding limited practical utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only five pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains many more persons (~24) and one vehicle, indicating major omissions.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides several attribute fields per person (actions, context, transporting, age) but fails to detect communicative group labels present in the ground truth, mislabels one transporting attribute, and misses a senior age, so coverage is partial with some incorrect values.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some coarse spatial cues (left sidewalk, right side, near center of crosswalk) are provided and align with the scene context, but they are not comprehensive or consistently associated with the many ground-truth persons and the vehicle is omitted.
justifications.overall: Significant missing detections and several incorrect or absent semantic labels make the extraction only partially useful for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The VLM reported only 6 pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists around 30 persons and one vehicle, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes (actions, context, carrying, age) are provided, but many important labels in the ground truth (group communication, looking into phone, running, senior age) and vehicle attributes are not captured.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few local relations are given (in front of, behind, next to, on left sidewalk) but they are sparse, do not cover most persons, and conflate crosswalk vs sidewalk positions in several cases.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some useful semantic labels but is largely incomplete and misses many entities and important attributes, limiting its overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected only four pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains dozens of persons and a parked vehicle, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic actions and simple contexts but misses many communicative states and age variations (child/senior) and includes at least one incorrect transporting label, so attribute coverage is sparse and partially incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few local relations (in front of/behind/near building) which are somewhat useful but are sparse, vaguely specified, and do not cover the majority of annotated people.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a small subset of pedestrians with minimal labels but omits most objects and many important semantic details, making it largely insufficient for reliable autonomous perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians and two vehicles while the ground truth lists over twenty people and multiple vehicles, so the extraction misses the vast majority of entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels like atomic actions and age for a couple of subjects but omits many communicative and transporting attributes and specific actions present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few coarse spatial relations that are plausible (on sidewalk, near crosswalk, parked along left sidewalk) but is sparse and does not cover most objects or finer relations.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and incomplete with limited semantic detail and spatial coverage compared to the dense ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected only five pedestrians and one vehicle, missing the majority of ground-truth persons and one vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic attributes per person (actions, context, age, transport) beyond the ground-truth schema but several labels are incorrect or mismatched (e.g., misidentified ages and a false carrying label).
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes coarse per-person spatial relations relative to the crosswalk, but these are vague, unvalidated, and many pedestrians are omitted so spatial coverage is poor.
justifications.overall: Extraction contains useful semantic fields but suffers from low recall and several incorrect attribute assignments, yielding limited reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Captured only 4 pedestrians and 2 vehicles versus over 30 persons and 5 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the vast majority of actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes multiple attribute fields (actions, context, transporting, age) for detected actors but omits many ground-truth labels and shows limited variety and depth across the scene.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reports a few local relations (near, on) that are sometimes correct but fails to capture many important relations and context distinctions such as jaywalking or walking on the road.
justifications.overall: Low detection recall and partial attribute coverage produce a poor overall extraction despite some correct attribute assignments for the few detected entities.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only five pedestrians and no vehicles despite the ground truth containing many more persons and a vehicle, so it misses the majority of entities and key context like jaywalking.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes several attribute fields (atomic_action, transporting, age, communicative) showing moderate richness, but many attributes are missing, incorrect, or not aligned with ground truth (e.g., jaywalking, carrying vs pulling).
justifications.spatial_relations: Some simple spatial cues are provided (near edge, on a bench), but many important spatial contexts are absent or incorrect (confuses sidewalk vs road and omits jaywalking/road positions).
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a few correct local attributes but is incomplete and contains notable semantic and spatial omissions and errors, yielding a low-to-moderate usefulness for driving perception evaluation.

justifications.completeness: Extraction lists only four pedestrians and no vehicles, missing the majority of ground-truth persons and the single vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attribute fields per detected pedestrian but misses many ground-truth labels and some transported/communicative labels are incorrect or absent (e.g., 'jaywalking', 'talking in group', 'looking into phone').
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial tags but fails to capture many critical relations and behaviors such as 'jaywalking' and 'walking on the road', using mostly generic terms like 'near the crosswalk' or 'on the sidewalk'.
justifications.overall: Overall low quality due to large omissions and some incorrect or missing attribute assignments despite a reasonable per-person schema.

justifications.completeness: Detected only four pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists 40+ persons and one vehicle, so there are major omissions.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes basic attributes (age, atomic_action, simple_context) but misses many contextual labels and several communicative/transporting annotations present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a few spatial tags (near the crosswalk, on the crosswalk, near the shops) but mislabels jaywalking pedestrians and omits many on-road/sidewalk relations.
justifications.overall: Gives a small correct subset with basic attributes but suffers from severe omissions and some incorrect spatial and context labels, limiting its usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Very low coverage of annotated entities: only four pedestrians listed versus many ground truth persons and no vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction reports multiple attribute types (actions, age, communicative, transporting) but misses many ground truth labels such as numerous carrying instances and vehicle attributes, so attributes are only partially captured.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some local spatial cues are provided but the extraction omits key context like jaywalking and lacks spatial relations for most annotated persons and all vehicles.
justifications.overall: The output captures basic attributes for a few people but is largely incomplete and misses critical semantics and vehicles, resulting in a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 3 pedestrians versus 15 in the ground truth and 4 vehicles matching the count but misses many pedestrian instances, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides several semantic attributes (actions, contexts, vehicle types and states) but fails to capture many specific contexts (multiple pedestrians crossing, a talking group) and has some inconsistent motion labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are present and plausible (near sidewalk, in middle, behind) but many pedestrians lack precise relations such as being at the pedestrian crossing and several relations are imprecise or incomplete.
justifications.overall: The extraction gives useful basic semantics for a few actors but omits the majority of pedestrians and several contextual details, limiting its overall reliability for scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Extraction detected only four pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains around twenty five people and two vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides basic atomic actions and ages for a few people but omits many annotated attributes such as transporting, communicative behaviors, complex contextual labels, and vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some local relations like near the store entrance and sidewalk edge are provided, but many important relations such as walking on the road, jaywalking, group interactions, and explicit vehicle placements are absent or incomplete.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a few correct pedestrian actions and coarse locations but fails to detect most entities and detailed semantics present in the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 4 pedestrians compared to 16 annotated persons and, while it lists 4 vehicles, it omits many pedestrian instances and some vehicle motion details, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes a wide set of semantic fields (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) providing rich descriptors, but several attribute assignments conflict with the ground truth or are missing finer contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few spatial relations are provided and plausibly placed, yet many important relations and distinctions (eg crossing vs walking along side, jaywalking locations) are absent or incorrect.
justifications.overall: The extraction is moderately informative due to diverse attribute fields but is substantially incomplete and contains multiple inaccuracies, yielding a below‑average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 4 pedestrians and 2 vehicles compared to 18 persons and 3 vehicles in the ground truth, missing many actors and some attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides multiple semantic labels (actions, contexts, transporting, age) giving moderate detail, but it also includes at least one incorrect transporting label and omits several communicative/contextual cues present in GT.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are captured (e.g., near truck, crossing) but many GT-specific relations (pedestrian crossing vs jaywalking, proximity to storefronts, etc.) are missing or under-specified.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction has useful semantic detail but is substantially incomplete and contains inaccuracies, resulting in a low-to-moderate quality assessment.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 4 of 26 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, so entity coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many attribute fields but missed several key labels (children ages, communicative group talking) and mislabelled transporting and vehicle motion, so richness is moderate but not reliably accurate.
justifications.spatial_relations: Included several plausible relations (in front of, near, next to) for extracted objects but omitted relations for most actors and had some uncertain assignments, yielding partial spatial understanding.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is partially informative but suffers from low recall of pedestrians and some incorrect attribute and motion assignments.

justifications.completeness: Detected only six pedestrians versus many annotated in the ground truth and omitted several context and age variations despite correctly listing both vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attribute fields (actions, simple context, transporting, age) showing moderate richness but misses communicative labels and misclassifies or omits child ages.
justifications.spatial_relations: Captures basic spatial relations and vehicle positions but misses numerous crossing, jaywalking, and finer relative relations present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains useful semantic elements and correct vehicle reporting but is substantially incomplete on pedestrian coverage and specific contextual cues, yielding moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 4 pedestrians and 2 vehicles while the ground truth contains dozens of persons and 3 vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many attribute fields (atomic_action, transporting, age, etc.) demonstrating semantic detail, but several attributes are missing or incorrect relative to the ground truth, reducing usefulness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are given (e.g., 'next to green bus', 'near white SUV', 'crossing in front of green bus') and a few crossings are identified, but many spatial contexts and jaywalking distinctions are missed or imprecise.
justifications.overall: Low completeness and errors in several semantic labels produce an overall low score despite moderate attribute richness.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses one annotated person and mislabels age and context for persons, and provides unsupported vehicle door/trunk states.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes many semantic attributes (actions, age, transport, spatial relations) but some attributes are incorrect or missing for one person.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relations like adjacency to the walkway and proximity to the railway are plausible but not verified in the ground truth and one asserted relation (fence) is unsupported.
justifications.overall: While the extraction captures several relevant attributes and vehicle motion, missing the second person and age/context errors reduce overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians and the vehicle but many attributes (ages, atomic actions, and vehicle motion) are incorrect or missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes a wide variety of semantic fields (actions, contexts, ages, vehicle type/status, spatial relations) even though several values are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided several plausible spatial relations that align with the underpass description, but some claims (vehicle outside/to the right) may be inaccurate relative to the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Overall it finds the main entities but significant semantic mistakes (wrong actions, ages, and vehicle motion) reduce the reliability of the extraction.

justifications.completeness: Extraction detects only a few pedestrians and two vehicles while the ground truth lists many more persons and three vehicle entries, so overall coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple attribute fields (actions, context, age, transporting, vehicle door/trunk states) indicating moderate richness, but several key labels are incorrect or missing compared to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided and partially match scene elements, but many agents lack spatial labels and the reported locations are imprecise or inconsistent with the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Limited coverage of agents combined with mixed attribute accuracy results in a below average extraction that is of limited use for complete scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists the correct number of people and a vehicle but misses or mislabels key ground-truth attributes such as both persons being seated seniors and the vehicle motion, so important ground-truth fields are incomplete or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides many semantic fields (age, actions, communicative, transporting, scene description) offering rich information, but several critical semantics are incorrect or contradicted by the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are well represented and consistent (person-person, person-bicycle, person-wall), which matches the visible scene layout even though ground truth does not explicitly list these relations.
justifications.overall: While the extraction captures entity presence and useful contextual details, significant errors in actions, ages, and vehicle motion reduce its reliability for safety-critical perception.

justifications.completeness: Partial: the model detects the cyclist, bike, and motion but omits the atomic action 'sitting', mislabels the subject's age (senior) as adult, and asserts trunk/door states not present in the annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attributes (bike basket, motion status, spatial relations) which add richness, but the presence of incorrect or unsupported claims (age, trunk/doors) reduces its semantic reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: It captures key spatial relations like being under an overpass and being ridden by a person that align with the scene, though the extra claim 'next to a concrete wall' is more specific than the annotations.
justifications.overall: Overall useful but imperfect: main objects and context are identified correctly, yet important errors in atomic action, age classification, and unsupported vehicle attributes limit accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Detected the person, atomic action, contexts, and absence of vehicles but missed the ground truth numeric track id and misclassified age as adult instead of senior.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic labels including action, contexts, age, and spatial relation, but the incorrect age label reduces semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Correctly identifies that the person is next to the underpass wall which aligns with the scene description.
justifications.overall: Good coverage of attributes and accurate spatial relation but the incorrect age classification and missing numeric track id lower the overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Correctly identifies the person and most attributes but misses the more specific age label and an object track id.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes key semantic attributes and adds spatial information but uses a less specific age category than the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a clear and plausible spatial relation that does not contradict the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Good coverage and semantic detail with a notable age misclassification reducing the score.

justifications.completeness: Captures the person's presence, action, and most contextual attributes but mislabels age (adult instead of senior) and omits a formal object track id.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful additional details (blue uniform, underpass) that enrich the annotation, but contains an incorrect age label which lowers semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Accurately describes the person's spatial relation to the underpass wall, which matches the scene context.
justifications.overall: Mostly correct and informative extraction with good spatial and contextual detail, but the incorrect age classification and missing formal track id reduce its reliability.
